Name: TBD: Australia Health AI Event Series
Description:
We are writing to invite your support for a groundbreaking series of Health AI workshops across Australia that will reimagine how artificial intelligence is developed, validated, and deployed in healthcare. These workshops will combine technical skill-building (LLM-a-thons, Health AI Systems Thinking for Equity sessions) with critical reflection on AI's societal impacts, centering voices typically excluded from technology development: First Nations communities, youth, patients, multicultural populations, and rural/remote practitioners. Rather than imposing solutions, our approach emphasizes co-design and participatory innovation, building agency and capacity within communities most affected by AI deployment while addressing urgent challenges like healthcare workforce shortages, data sovereignty, climate impacts on health, and algorithmic bias.

The workshops address a critical gap as private AI companies increasingly shape Australia's health systems with minimal attention to ethics, equity, or community values. The event will bring together diverse stakeholders—from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander elders and youth to clinicians, computer scientists, medical students, bioethicists, and digital health researchers—to examine concrete questions: How do we safeguard against bias in clinical algorithms? What does data sovereignty mean for First Nations communities? How can AI support rather than displace rural healthcare access? What guardrails prevent unintended harms? By starting with reflection and reimagination before technical implementation, and by training youth as "double agents" who can navigate existing power structures while plotting transformative alternatives, we aim to ensure that Australia's AI future is shaped through us, not done to us. We seek support for venues, catering, participant stipends (particularly for community members who would otherwise lose wages), and materials. Your sponsorship would directly enable communities to claim their seat at the table where AI's future in Australian healthcare is being decided.
Event Lead/Organizer: Multiple people: Group chat with people from multiple Australian states

Name: Brown University DSAIY AI-a-Thon
Description: The AI-a-thon brings together high school students, teachers, data scientists, and healthcare professionals for a hands-on, collaborative experience exploring how artificial intelligence is used in healthcare. Working in cross-disciplinary teams, participants tackle real-world activities that reveal how AI works, where it can go wrong, and why bias, accuracy, and verification matter. Just as importantly, the event centers youth voices, inviting students to share what they think about AI and how it may shape their futures, communities, and careers. March 19-20th, 2025
Location: Brown University: 225 Dyer St. Providence, RI
Other Team members: Ahram Han, Boya Zhang, Laurine, Marlene, Takeshi, Ryo Hei, Rawan, Raul, Tiger Krittaphas, Taha, Mackenzie Meni, Sonya, Evan, Hannes, Rahul
